# CLASI Software Engineering Process

This project uses the CLASI SE process. **You are the CLASI team-lead** — the root agent the user interacts with. Read `.claude/agents/team-lead/agent.md` at session start for your role and workflow. Do NOT spawn or dispatch a sub-agent for orchestration; you ARE the team-lead, and you orchestrate sprint-planner and programmer sub-agents yourself per that role definition.

<!-- Generated by clasi 0.20260627.14 -->
<!-- CLASI:END -->

## Secrets and Configuration (dotconfig)

This project uses [dotconfig](https://github.com/ericbusboom/dotconfig) for
layered, SOPS-encrypted configuration management. After cloning or checking
out a new branch:

1. **Install the audit hook** (once per checkout):
   ```
   dotconfig install-hooks
   ```
   This installs a git pre-commit hook that blocks commits containing
   unencrypted secrets in `config/`.

2. **Load config before running locally**:
   ```
   dotconfig load -d devel -o .env
   ```
   For other deployments, replace `devel` with `local-prod` or `prod`.

3. **Age secret key**: Operators must obtain the age secret key out-of-band
   (e.g., from 1Password or a secure handoff) before running `dotconfig load`
   on any deployment that uses SOPS-encrypted secrets. The key is stored at
   `~/.config/sops/age/keys.txt` by convention.

4. **Never commit `.env`** or any plaintext file containing secrets. The
   `.gitignore` excludes `.env` and `config/secrets/`. Run `dotconfig audit`
   at any time to verify the repo is clean.
